mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate, compressing present values of future equity cash flows and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor demand and impending earnings contraction, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where the unemployment trigger activates.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in housing starts and retail sales confirms robust consumer spending and business investment, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when both housing starts and retail sales increase simultaneously for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during supply-constrained periods indicates firm pricing power and margin expansion, boosting short-term equity valuations over the next week.
failure_condition: Equity returns decline when industrial production rises during periods of severe supply chain disruption and input cost spikes.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding 3.5 percent annually forces market pricing of persistent hawkish monetary policy, depressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive when inflation consistently trades above the 3.5 percent threshold for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in nonfarm payrolls growth and real GDP expansion confirms broad economic resilience, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when employment and GDP growth accelerate concurrently during periods of elevated corporate leverage.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales consistently lead equity market momentum as consumer spending drives near-term corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Equity returns turn negative when retail sales increase for three consecutive months without corresponding earnings growth.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets, a rising unemployment rate acts as a leading indicator of earnings contraction, depressing equity returns over the next month.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive forward returns when unemployment rises during periods of strong corporate profit margins and low recession probabilities.
